data.title: Pi Coin Experiences 3.26% Decline Amid Bearish Market Setup
data.description: Pi Coin's price fell by 3.26% to $0.178, raising concerns about a potential further decline. According to NS3.AI, the current market conditions resemble those of December, suggesting a possible 33.11% drop to $0.130. The analysis highlighted that the Chaikin Money Flow (CMF) indicator has decreased to -0.11, while the Money Flow Index (MFI) is at 35.23. A daily close above $0.210 could negate the bearish outlook.

data.title: Bittensor's TAO Declines 6.97% After Brief Surge
data.description: Bittensor's TAO experienced a 6.97% decline, trading at $322 after a brief surge to $380 on March 26. According to NS3.AI, the breakout above the $306 Fibonacci resistance zone has stalled. Market sentiment has weakened, and liquidation leverage has clustered at $364.

data.title: UN Warns of Potential Trade Disruptions in Hormuz Strait Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: The United Nations has expressed concerns over the escalating situation in the Hormuz Strait, which could disrupt maritime trade in the coming months. According to Odaily, UN Secretary-General spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric highlighted the potential chain reactions affecting humanitarian needs and agricultural production due to ongoing Middle East conflicts. Despite Secretary-General António Guterres's commitment to a comprehensive and lasting resolution, immediate action is crucial to mitigate these consequences. In response to these challenges, the Secretary-General has established a dedicated task force.

data.title: Iran Issues Evacuation Warning Amid Tensions with U.S. and Israel
data.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has issued a warning to personnel at industrial enterprises linked to the U.S. and Israel, advising them to leave immediately. According to NS3.AI, residents within a one-kilometer radius have also been urged to evacuate temporarily during relevant operations. This warning comes in response to several strikes by the U.S. and Israel on Iranian industrial centers earlier that day, prompting Iran to announce its decision to undertake retaliatory actions.

data.title: The UN Secretary-General has established a special working group to address the situation in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: On March 27 local time, UN Secretary-General's spokesperson, Stéphane Dujarric, stated regarding the situation in the Strait of Hormuz that as the Middle East conflict continues to develop and could escalate further, the disruption of maritime trade through the Strait of Hormuz could trigger a chain reaction in the coming months, impacting humanitarian needs and agricultural production. While Secretary-General Guterres is committed to making every effort to achieve a comprehensive and lasting solution to the conflict, immediate action to mitigate these consequences is crucial. To address these challenges, the Secretary-General has established a dedicated working group. (CCTV News)

data.title: Shares of Strategy, BitMine, and Robinhood fell to monthly lows, while Bitcoin dropped to its lowest level since March 2.
data.description: Bitcoin fell to its lowest price since March 2nd on Friday, reaching $65,720, with recent trading around $65,804, a drop of over 4% on the day. Ethereum fell about 4% to $1,980, Solana fell 5% to below $83, and BNB fell 3% to $608. According to CoinGlass data, over $500 million in crypto positions were liquidated in the past 24 hours, with nearly 90% being long positions. Strategy, the largest corporate Bitcoin holder with approximately $50 billion in Bitcoin, saw its stock, MSTR, fall over 5% on the day, breaking below $124 intraday, a one-month low. BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR), the largest publicly traded company with the largest Ethereum holdings, touched a one-month low of $18.42 intraday, a drop of over 4% on the day. Cryptocurrency and stock trading platform Robinhood (HOOD) also fell to a monthly low, trading slightly above $66, down more than 11% over the past month and more than 50% over the last six months. All three major U.S. stock indices fell, with the Nasdaq down 1.5%, and the S&P 500 and Dow Jones each down about 1%. The U.S. President said after the market closed on Thursday that he would suspend plans to strike Iranian energy facilities, but Israel subsequently said it would "escalate" its attacks on Iran following a missile strike. Users of the prediction market platform Myriad currently believe there is a 64% probability that Bitcoin's next target is $55,000 rather than $84,000.

data.title: Iran warns personnel associated with US and Israeli industrial companies in the region to evacuate immediately.
data.description: The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a statement saying that Iran has decided to retaliate after multiple attacks by the United States and Israel on Iranian industrial facilities. The statement warned industrial enterprises and heavy industry workers in the region with ties to the US and Israel to immediately evacuate their workplaces to avoid endangering their lives. The statement indicated that despite previous warnings from Iran urging against action against Iranian industrial facilities, the US and Israel carried out multiple strikes on Iranian industrial centers that day. The statement also called on residents within a one-kilometer radius of such industrial facilities to temporarily evacuate their homes during the attacks. (CCTV News)

data.title: U.S. Secretary of State Discusses Iran Strategy Without Ground Troops
data.description:
U.S. Secretary of State Rubio stated on March 28 that the United States can achieve its objectives in Iran without deploying ground forces. According to BlockBeats, there have been exchanges of information with Iran, and indications suggest a willingness from the Iranian side to negotiate on certain issues.

PolyBeats monitoring reveals that on the prediction market Polymarket, the probability of U.S. ground forces entering Iran by the end of March stands at 18%, while the likelihood of entry by the end of April is 61%.

data.title: Iran May Implement Toll System in Strait of Hormuz, Says U.S. Secretary of State Rubio
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio has indicated that Iran might establish a toll system in the Strait of Hormuz. According to Odaily, this development could have significant implications for international shipping and trade routes. The Strait of Hormuz is a strategic chokepoint for global oil transportation, and any changes to its accessibility could impact global markets. The potential toll system reflects ongoing geopolitical tensions in the region, with Iran seeking to exert more control over this critical maritime passage.

data.title: Mastercard Acquires BVNK for $1.8 Billion in Historic Stablecoin Deal
data.description: Mastercard has acquired stablecoin infrastructure company BVNK for $1.8 billion, marking a significant move in the digital currency space. According to NS3.AI, this acquisition is more than double BVNK's $750 million Series B valuation from just over a year ago. BVNK has developed enterprise-grade stablecoin settlement rails across 130 jurisdictions, making this transaction the largest stablecoin infrastructure deal in history.

data.title: Senator Warren Seeks Documents on Bitmain Amid National Security Concerns
data.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren has requested documents from the U.S. Commerce Department regarding Bitmain, a Chinese Bitcoin mining equipment manufacturer, due to potential national security concerns. According to NS3.AI, this request follows a Bloomberg News report from November indicating that Bitmain was the subject of a federal investigation. Bitmain is noted for selling the majority of the world's Bitcoin-mining rigs.

data.title: U.S. Secretary of State Predicts End of Iran Conflict Within Weeks
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio has informed his G7 counterparts that the conflict in Iran is expected to conclude within a few weeks. According to Odaily, three informed sources have confirmed this development.

data.title: US Secretary of State: Iran may decide to establish a toll collection system in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: US Secretary of State Rubio: Iran may decide to establish a toll collection system in the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Paulson Stresses Importance of Stable Long-term Inflation Expectations
data.description: Federal Reserve official Paulson emphasized the critical importance of maintaining stable long-term inflation expectations. According to ChainCatcher, this statement highlights the ongoing focus of the Federal Reserve on ensuring economic stability amid fluctuating market conditions.

data.title: Genius Terminal has entered into a strategic partnership with Aster, allowing users to trade Aster perpetual contracts at lower fees.
data.description: Genius and Aster have announced a strategic partnership, with Genius becoming a core distribution partner for the Aster Chain launch. Through this partnership, Genius receives preferential terms, allowing users to trade Aster perpetual contracts through Genius at lower fees than using the Aster platform directly. Genius is a multi-chain trading terminal that has processed over $60 million in transactions across more than 10 chains, including BNB Chain, Solana, and Ethereum, within five weeks of its launch. Genius has also been selected for Aster's "Aster Code" program, which rewards partners who bring real value to the Aster Chain launch. Aster is a privacy-focused on-chain trading platform offering perpetual contract trading in cryptocurrencies, stocks, and commodities, powered by the Layer 1 blockchain Aster Chain, both supported by YZi Labs.

data.title: Iran says it may retaliate by striking steel industry facilities in Israel and several other countries in the region.
data.description: On March 27, local time, it was learned from Iranian sources that following the attacks by the United States and Israel on Iranian steel plants, Iran has designated six steel plants in Israel and related industrial facilities in five other countries in the region as new targets for retaliatory strikes. According to informed sources, Iran's response is likely not limited to the regional steel industry and may involve "broader and stronger" measures. (CCTV News)

data.title: Sources say US Secretary of State Rubio said the war with Iran should end within weeks.
data.description: Market news: Three sources familiar with the matter said that US Secretary of State Rubio told his G7 colleagues that the war with Iran should end within weeks. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran Prepares for Potential Ground Conflict Amid U.S. Military Buildup
data.description: Iran has announced its readiness to counter any form of ground invasion as the U.S. continues to increase its military presence in the Middle East. According to Odaily, Iranian media reported on March 26 that Iran has mobilized over one million combat personnel in preparation for a potential ground conflict with the United States.

data.title: Iran Threatens to Target Steel Plants in Gulf Region and Israel
data.description: Iran has issued a warning about potential attacks on steel plants located in the Gulf region and Israel. According to Odaily, this announcement was reported by Iran's Tasnim News Agency. The warning highlights escalating tensions in the region, raising concerns over the security of industrial facilities.

data.title: Nouriel Roubini Warns of Potential U.S.-Iran Conflict Impact on Federal Reserve Rates
data.description: Economist Nouriel Roubini has expressed concerns about the possibility of U.S. President Donald Trump expanding military actions against Iran. According to NS3.AI, Roubini estimates there is a greater than 50% chance of such an escalation occurring. He cautioned that this could lead to a situation reminiscent of the stagflation experienced in the 1970s, potentially prompting the Federal Reserve to consider a rate hike.
